title: Thelypteris subarida
taxonomy: Thelypteris subarida is classified within the plant kingdom as a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. This family consists of over 1,000 species of ferns, and Thelypteris subarida is one of the many species within it. The genus Thelypteris is further divided into several species, with Thelypteris subarida being one of the lesser-known species within this group. The classification of Thelypteris subarida is also known as the subarid fern, due to its unique characteristics and adaptations. Further classification and research are needed to fully understand the evolutionary history and relationships of Thelypteris subarida within the plant kingdom.

intro: Thelypteris subarida, also known as the narrow-leaved maiden fern, is a species of flowering plant in the family Thelypteridaceae, characterized by its delicate, lance-shaped fronds and adaptable growth habits in various environments. The health benefits of Thelypteris subarida include its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing oxidative stress and alleviating symptoms of certain diseases.
